title: Current Thoughts on Té Amo — Tea and Art House
html:
TL;DR

  • Operational follow-up to Té Amo research dump — proposes a 2-month pop-up (farmers markets, art shows, events) as the low risk practice run before any brick-and-mortar
  • Co-operator named: jacoby — first vault appearance of Té Amo's second person; Yahir explicitly worries about "sustainability of myself and jacoby running it"
  • Menu architecture: 30+ herbs/teas (hierba buena, manzanilla, mullein, elderberry, arnica, hibiscus, lemongrass, yerba mate, cacao tea), horchata combos (matcha, chai), no-Nestlé Latin American hot chocolate sourcing requirement
  • Pricing locked in: $4.50 drip/tea → $6 cacao/yerba mate, $5–9 food, "create your own blend" $5 + $1 per add-in, group pot $15 — full unit economics drafted
  • Considerations checklist: 1000 sq ft target, food-handler/entertainment/public-health permits, LLC, resale cert, suppliers, ceramicists, bakery partner — full pre-launch operating worry-list

Notes

Written one day after the resource dump doc, this is Yahir thinking through Té Amo as an actual operation. The pop-up framing is the strategically interesting move: rather than commit to brick-and-mortar (the romantic version of a tea-and-art house), Yahir reframes it as a distribution-test before commitment — "if it tanks, good to know and quit investing without so much of a loss." This is the same 60 day activation / no runway discipline instinct showing up in a pre-software product.

Six pop-up rationales spelled out: raise funds, market research, build Instagram presence, network into target community, low-risk practice, sustainable 1-day-a-week cadence post-launch.

The jacoby mention is significant — first time a Té Amo co-operator appears by name. Worth checking 2024 raws to see if jacoby stays or churns. The food architecture has a clear cultural intent (conchas, lentejas, caldo de pollo, sopa de fideo, sopa de verduras, avocado toast) — Té Amo is a Mexican American third place, not a generic tea shop, and the no-Nestlé hot chocolate rule is the giveaway.

Companion: 2023 12 29 te amo tea and art house. Worth densifying once Yahir's Austin move recontextualizes the LA-anchored project.
